channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Lula participa de entrega simultânea de moradias do Minha Casa, Minha Vida
item.description: President Lula (PT) participated in the simultaneous delivery of 2,215 housing units under the Minha Casa, Minha Vida program across four states (Pará, Bahia, Alagoas, and the Federal District), benefiting over 8,800 people. The ceremony took place at the Palácio do Planalto, with coordinated events in Santarém (PA), Dias d’Ávila (BA), Rio Largo (AL), and São Brás (AL), involving Minister of Cities Jader Filho and funding from the Residential Leasing Fund and the Union’s General Budget.

item.title: "Não entendi o movimento", diz Flávio sobre Ratinho Jr. no Planalto
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) said he did not understand Governor Ratinho Júnior's (PSD-PR) presidential pre-candidacy, stating there was no reason for the move. He praised the governor as a 'great leader' and 'good figure' for national alliances, but said the withdrawal was surprising. Flávio also commented on Sergio Moro's PL affiliation and Moro's selection as the party's pre-candidate for governor of Paraná.

item.title: Não é a guerra que amedronta os economistas do Brasil, mas um outro perigo real
item.description: The Copom minutes were uneventful, but economists’ real concern is the potential testimony of Daniel Vorcaro, seen as a direct threat to the Brazilian real and assets, capable of destabilizing expectations amid tense elections. André Perfeito advocates aggressive rate cuts, arguing current credit conditions are silently eroding businesses and households, while Ricardo Rocha agrees with the diagnosis but opposes off-cycle moves, citing the BC’s inherent caution amid political volatility. The Middle East conflict has receded in relevance as domestic political risk now poses a more immediate and direct threat to exchange rates, inflation, and interest rates.

item.title: Com interino no comando, RJ fará eleição para mandato tampão; entenda
item.description: The resignation of Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) led to Judge Ricardo Couto assuming the interim governorship of Rio de Janeiro, following the vacancy of the vice-governor and the absence of the Alerj president. An indirect election (buffer mandate) will be held within 30 days to select a governor to serve until the October 2026 inauguration. The TSE resumed proceedings that could result in Castro’s removal and eight-year ineligibility for alleged abuse of economic and political power in the 2022 re-election campaign.

item.title: Ratinho Jr, desiste de ser candidato e Caiado deve sair a presidente pelo PSD
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Jr. withdrew his candidacy for president under the PSD, opening space for Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado to become the party's presidential nominee. The decision followed a meeting of PSD leaders in São Paulo, including national party president Gilberto Kassab. The news was reported by UOL on March 24, 2026, emphasizing its impact on the opposition's electoral strategy.

item.title: Caiado quer colocar o bloco na rua já
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado traveled urgently to São Paulo to meet with Gilberto Kassab, president of the PSD, to discuss his potential 2026 presidential candidacy. The meeting will cover party structure, campaign planning, and financing for Caiado’s political project. The encounter was confirmed by O Antagonista.

item.title: Moro insinua que vitória de Lula sobre Bolsonaro foi ilegítima
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro insinuated without providing evidence that Lula's victory over Bolsonaro in the 2022 election was illegitimate during his affiliation with the PL on March 24, 2026. He claimed the current president is on the side of criminals and minimizes crime, repeating Bolsonarist narratives despite his Lava Jato convictions being annulled by the STF in 2021. The statement was made alongside Flávio Bolsonaro and Valdemar Costa Neto, marking his formal alignment with bolsonarismo as a pré-candidate for governor of Paraná.

item.title: STF rejeita novos recursos do núcleo de desinformação da trama golpista
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) unanimously rejected appeals from the seven defendants in the disinformation nucleus of the coup plot, upholding their convictions for involvement in the post-2022 election attempt to overturn democracy, including spreading fake news and misusing the Abin to monitor authorities. The defendants were found to have sought to create political instability to justify military intervention and keep Jair Bolsonaro in power.

item.title: Edinho sobre Flávio moderado: "É legítimo quererem construir um tipo"
item.description: The national president of the Workers' Party (PT), Edinho Silva, described Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) moderate profile in the pre-campaign as a 'marketing strategy', stating it is legitimate for candidates to want to 'build an image' during elections, but emphasized voters must examine the candidate's history. He argued that Flávio represents the Bolsonaro family legacy and warned against forgetting Brazil under Bolsonaro's governance, despite current rebranding efforts.

item.title: O que novas pesquisas revelam sobre Lula x Flávio na região que define os rumos da eleição
item.description: Recent Real Time Big Data polls show a tight race between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in the Southeast, which holds 42% of the electorate and is pivotal for the 2026 election. In Minas Gerais and São Paulo, the candidates are technically tied, while Flávio leads narrowly in Rio de Janeiro and Espírito Santo. The contest is increasingly driven by voter rejection rather than policy proposals, with Lula burdened by corruption scandals and Flávio facing challenges in solidifying his base.

item.title: Bolsonaro está internado recebendo antibioticoterapia, sem previsão de deixar hospital, diz boletim
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro remains hospitalized at DF Star since March 13 due to bilateral bacterial pneumonia, receiving intravenous antibiotic therapy and clinical support. There is no forecast for discharge, and he is serving a 27-year-and-three-month sentence for attempting a coup to stay in power after the 2022 elections. Allies and the Prosecutor General’s Office are pushing for house arrest, with Minister Alexandre de Moraes of the STF set to decide, following a favorable opinion from the PGR.

item.title: Michelle faz publicação enigmática após encontro com Moraes
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Bolsonaro posted an enigmatic message on Instagram after visiting Minister Alexandre de Moraes, stating that 'not every provocation deserves a response'. The meeting followed a favorable opinion from the PGR regarding house arrest for Jair Bolsonaro, who is hospitalized with bronchopneumonia. Allies interpreted the post as an indirect comment on differences in her approach compared to Flávio Bolsonaro in defending the former president.

item.title: Em filiaçăo ao PL, Moro diz a Flávio Bolsonaro: 'Paraná năo vai lhe faltar'
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro, upon joining the Liberal Party (PL) in Brasília, stated he will provide his political platform in Paraná to support Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) 2026 presidential campaign. The remark was made during his affiliation ceremony, indicating political alignment between the two.

item.title: Quem é Ricardo Couto, que assume governo do Rio após renúncia de Castro
item.description: Judge Ricardo Couto assumed the governorship of Rio de Janeiro following the resignation of Cláudio Castro, who faced potential removal by the TSE over alleged abuse of power in the 2022 elections. Couto, president of the Rio State Court of Justice, will serve only until an indirect election in the Legislative Assembly determines an interim governor, without planning administrative changes or seeking to extend his term, amid judicial uncertainty over succession rules and the STF's pending decision.

item.title: ‘Verão do Flávio’ pulveriza favoritismo de Lula
item.description: The 'Summer of Flávio' marks the strengthening of Flávio Bolsonaro's pre-candidacy, with rising poll numbers and consolidated bolsonarist support, while Lula faces declining popularity, scandals like the Master case, and internal PT challenges. A potential house arrest for Jair Bolsonaro could further unify the bolsonarist base behind Flávio, and the rise of Ronaldo Caiado as a 'third way' alternative adds complexity to the 2026 electoral landscape.

item.title: Deltan Dallagnol é anunciado pré-candidato ao Senado pelo Paraná em evento de filiação de Sergio Moro ao PL
item.description: Deltan Dallagnol was announced as a pre-candidate for the Senate from Paraná during Sergio Moro's affiliation event with the PL, forming a 'Lava Jato' ticket. Despite having his candidacy registration annulled in 2023, Dallagnol remains eligible to run in 2026. The event also confirmed Filipe Barros as a Senate candidate on the same ticket and marked the PL's break with Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior, who withdrew his presidential pre-candidacy.

item.title: Silvia Abravanel se filia ao PSD para concorrer a deputada federal em SP
item.description: Television presenter Silvia Abravanel, daughter of Silvio Santos, is joining the PSD to run for federal deputy from São Paulo. The affiliation ceremony will take place this Tuesday at 3 p.m. at the party’s headquarters in São Paulo. She follows her father’s political path, who ran for president in 1989 under the PMB, a candidacy later invalidated by the TSE due to party registration irregularities and his ownership of a public service concession.

item.title: Davi Kopenawa cobra julgamento no TSE que pode cassar Denarium
item.description: Indigenous leader Davi Kopenawa delivered a letter to TSE President Cármen Lúcia demanding the urgent judgment of a case that could remove Roraima Governor Antonio Denarium for abuse of power in the 2022 elections. He links Denarium’s continued tenure to the expansion of illegal mining in Yanomami territory, which brings disease, death, mercury contamination, and environmental destruction. The case, initiated in 2024 at the TSE, has been stalled by requests for review despite a favorable vote for cassation by relator Isabel Gallotti in August 2025.

item.title: Lula foi eleito “entre aspas” e apoia criminosos, diz Moro
item.description: Senator Sérgio Moro launched his pre-candidacy for governor of Paraná and officially joined the PL party, claiming Lula was elected 'in quotes' and is aligned with criminals, while criticizing the government's economy as chaotic and claiming corruption has returned on an unimaginable scale, even stealing from retirees. Moro also said he expects Jair Bolsonaro to be placed under house arrest for 'a matter of justice' and reiterated the need to defeat Lula, stating his election brought a shadow over the country worse than he had imagined.

item.title: Em filiação de Moro ao PL, Flávio Bolsonaro diz que Lula 'passa mão na cabeça de bandido'
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president, criticized President Lula for allegedly 'patting bandits on the head' during Sergio Moro's affiliation event with the PL. He reaffirmed his intention to harden the public security agenda, arguing that 'marginals' must be treated firmly.

item.title: Em filiação de Moro ao PL, Flávio Bolsonaro diz que Lula ‘passa mão na cabeça de bandido’
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) criticized President Lula, claiming his government 'pampers criminals' and advocated for a tougher public security agenda. He reaffirmed his support for Sergio Moro’s affiliation with the PL and announced alliances with Filipe Barros and Deltan Dallagnol for Senate seats in Paraná, emphasizing the state’s strategic importance for his 2026 presidential bid.

item.title: Arrecadação com casas de apostas sobe 236% em 2026, diz Fisco
item.description: Brazil's revenue from betting houses surged 236% in the first two months of 2026, reaching R$ 2.5 billion, driven by the full implementation of the fixed-odds betting market regulation since 2025. The increase reflects an expanded tax base and improved oversight by the Federal Revenue Service, with the tax on Gross Gaming Revenue (GGR) rising from 12% to 13% in 2026, as approved by Congress through PLP 128/2025.

item.title: Por dentro da suposta operação russa para fomentar protestos contra o governo em Angola
item.description: Two Russians, Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov, were arrested in Angola in August 2023 and now face 11 charges, including terrorism, espionage, and influence peddling, for alleged involvement in an operation by Africa Politology, a network linked to the defunct Wagner Group, aimed at fomenting protests and interfering in Angola’s 2024 presidential election. The defense denies any ties to the Russian state or Wagner, claiming the men were merely working to establish a 'Russian House' cultural center in Luanda. The case underscores rising tensions between Russia and Angola, a major oil and diamond producer that has been gradually distancing itself from Russian influence since the Cold War.

item.title: Flávio lança Moro e diz ser o verdadeiro defensor da democracia
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro launched Sérgio Moro as the PL's pre-candidate for governor of Paraná and announced Moro's official affiliation with the party. He claimed his political group represents the "true defenders of democracy" and criticized the Lula government, alleging it is delivering more misery, dependence, and corruption. Flávio stressed alignment with Moro on public security, emphasizing crime-fighting as central to the state race.

item.title: Moro se filia ao PL para concorrer ao governo do Paraná e promete palanque forte a Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Senator Sérgio Moro officially joined the Liberal Party (PL) to run for governor of Paraná in 2026, promising continuity with change and offering a strong campaign platform for presidential pre-candidate Flávio Bolsonaro. The event featured PL president Valdemar Costa Neto, Felipe Barros, and Deltan Dallagnol — announced as Moro’s Senate candidate — forming a ‘Lava Jato’ ticket. Moro’s move signals the PL’s break with Governor Ratinho Junior, who abandoned his presidential bid following this development.

item.title: Investigação do Banco Master volta a encostar em autoridades com foro
item.description: New PF investigations reveal evidence of involvement by authorities with privileged forum in the Banco Master scandal, ruling out the possibility of sending the case to first instance. Minister André Mendonça, rapporteur at the STF, is expected to issue new measures in the coming weeks, while former CEO Daniel Vorcaro signed a confidentiality agreement to begin plea bargaining, whose legal weight depends on independent corroboration.

item.title: A mudança na federação União-PP após a desistência de Ratinho Junior
item.description: The withdrawal of Ratinho Jr. from the presidential race has opened the path for Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) to pursue the presidency with Gilberto Kassab's (União Brasil) backing, aiming to form a 'pure blood' Centrão ticket with Tereza Cristina (PP-MS) as vice-president. The effort includes talks between Kassab, Ciro Nogueira (PP) and Antônio Rueda (União Brasil) to secure party support, despite internal fragmentation and conflicting alignments with the PT in certain states.

item.title: Com Lula em 8º, confira o ranking dos presidentes mais populares da América Latina
item.description: Lula (PT) ranks as the eighth most popular president in Latin America in March 2026, with 46.6% positive image, according to a CB Consultora Opinión Pública survey. He dropped from sixth place in February, while Claudia Sheinbaum (Mexico) leads the ranking with 72.3% approval. The first South American on the list is Bolivian Rodrigo Paz, ranked sixth overall with 51.9%.

item.title: Folha secreta do Ceperj: entenda denúncia contra Cláudio Castro no TSE
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) faces trial at the TSE over alleged use of a 'secret payroll' at Ceperj and Uerj during the 2022 campaign, involving 27,000 and 18,000 temporary positions respectively to hire election operatives. Castro resigned one day before the trial resumed, asserting confidence in electoral justice and denying wrongdoing after the campaign began. The MP-RJ reports a 2,139% increase in Ceperj spending from 2020 to 2022, which could result in ineligibility until 2030 if convicted.

item.title: O que resta para o TSE concluir julgamento que pode tornar Cláudio Castro inelegível
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) resumes the trial that could render former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro ineligible for eight years, based on the use of public structure in the 2022 campaign. So far, two ministers have voted for conviction, with votes still pending from Kassio Nunes Marques, Floriano de Azevedo Marques, Estela Aranha, André Mendonça, and President Cármen Lúcia. The case centers on allegations of 27,000 temporary hires at the state-funded Fundação Ceperj used to boost Castro’s re-election bid, allegedly constituting an illegal diversion of public resources for electoral gain.

item.title: Alta do IOF faz governo Lula arrecadar 41,8% a mais com o tributo
item.description: The Lula government recorded a 41.8% increase in IOF tax revenue in the first two months of 2026, reaching R$16.8 billion, driven by higher rates and an expanded tax base under Decree 12.499/25. Despite opposition from Congress and financial markets, the government preserved most of the increase following STF rulings, with Justice Alexandre de Moraes upholding the decree and rejecting the congressional repeal. The IOF remains a key tool to strengthen federal finances amid slowing revenues from foreign trade.

item.title: Conselho do FGTS aprova ampliação de renda do Minha Casa Minha Vida
item.description: The FGTS Council approved the expansion of income brackets for the Minha Casa Minha Vida program, allowing families earning up to R$13,000 to participate. The changes include raising income limits across all brackets and increasing the maximum financed property value, benefiting around 127,000 families through lower interest rates and new eligibility.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula faz entrega do Minha Casa, Minha Vida Rural no Planalto
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is participating in the simultaneous delivery of 2,215 homes under the Minha Casa, Minha Vida Rural program, with a ceremony at the Palácio do Planalto and live broadcast to Santarém (PA), Dias d’Avila (BA), Rio Largo (AL), and São Braz (AL). The largest batch, comprising 1,408 units, will be delivered in Santarém with the presence of Minister of Cities Jader Filho. The event takes place on Tuesday, March 24, 2026, at 11:00 a.m., underscoring the government’s commitment to rural public housing.

item.title: Saída de Castro reconfigura disputa no Rio e embaralha plano bolsonarista
item.description: The resignation of Governor Cláudio Castro reconfigures the Bolsonarist strategy in Rio, following an STF decision that barred Douglas Ruas from running for state governor due to insufficient disaffiliation. Castro aims to avoid ineligibility by resigning early, preserving his Senate candidacy. The Bolsonarist group has not yet chosen a new gubernatorial candidate, while allies of Eduardo Paes see an opportunity to field a competitive alternative.

item.title: O que está por trás do terno marrom que Lula passou a usar em eventos
item.description: President Lula drew attention by wearing a brown suit at a recent event at the Palácio do Planalto, breaking from his usual preference for somber tones like navy, black, and gray. The choice aligns with Pantone’s global selection of 'Mocha Mousse' as the Color of the Year, which has boosted the shade’s visibility in fashion, and also reflects his broader image repositioning following a professional weight-loss journey. The outfit was worn during the announcement of measures to mitigate fuel price impacts amid international tensions over oil.

item.title: Lula deve vetar trecho e sancionar restante do PL Antifacção, segundo auxiliares
item.description: President Lula is expected to sign the Anti-Crime Bill into law with only one veto, removing a provision that would have extended penalties to individuals associated with criminal factions, due to concerns over the criminalization of social movements. The signing ceremony will take place this Tuesday at 3 p.m. at the Planalto Palace, attended by Chamber President Hugo Motta, while Senate President Davi Alcolumbre is invited but not expected to attend. Simultaneously, the government is preparing a decree with six strategic axes to regulate the law, though its release has been postponed to next week.

item.title: Governo de SP amplia monitoramento ambiental por satélite
item.description: The São Paulo state government, under Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicans), expanded its environmental monitoring capacity ninefold using satellite imagery and geospatial analysis to detect changes in native vegetation and guide enforcement actions. The initiative aims to strengthen environmental protection across the state.

item.title: Ronaldo Caiado se reúne com Kassab em São Paulo após Ratinho Jr. desistir de disputar a Presidência
item.description: Goias Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) met with PSD national president Gilberto Kassab in São Paulo following the withdrawal of Ratinho Júnior from the party's presidential nomination race. Caiado has gained traction as a potential candidate after Ratinho Júnior's exit, while Eduardo Leite also vies for the position. Although not a consensus, Caiado is viewed by party figures as the likely favorite due to his experience, stance against seeking re-election, and strong internal polling.

item.title: Designer da camisa da seleção diz que torcedor chama Brasil de Brasa
item.description: Nike designer Rachel Denti explained that the term 'Brasa' on Brazil's 2026 World Cup jerseys is something she claims to hear in stadiums and on the streets, though fans criticized it as not being a common way to refer to Brazil. The inclusion sparked controversy on social media, with some users linking the term to left-wing politics and the Lula government.

item.title: Prefeito e primeira-dama do interior de SP morrem em acidente de carro
item.description: The mayor of Vista Alegre do Alto, Nelson Rozani (Podemos), and his wife, First Lady Fátima Rozani, died in a car accident on Monday night. The couple left three children and were mourned with official mourning declared by the city hall. Governor Tarcísio de Freitas expressed sorrow and solidarity with the family and the community.

item.title: Após desistência de Ratinho Jr, Kassab se encontra com Ronaldo Caiado em SP para falar de candidatura à presidência
item.description: Following Ratinho Júnior’s withdrawal from the presidential race, PSD national president Gilberto Kassab met with Goiás governor Ronaldo Caiado in São Paulo to discuss the party’s presidential nominee. Kassab also plans to meet with Rio Grande do Sul governor Eduardo Leite, who recently joined the PSD and has expressed interest in leading the ticket. The party aims to announce its candidate by the end of March, emphasizing a centrist alternative beyond political polarization.

item.title: Romário critica Ancelotti e defende Neymar na seleção: ‘Craque tem que jogar’
item.description: Romário criticized Carlo Ancelotti's decision to leave Neymar out of the Brazil national team, stating that 'a star player must play' and that Neymar's talent should not be wasted, even if not at peak fitness. Ancelotti insisted Neymar would only be considered if 100% physically fit, following recent knee issues. The former player affirmed he would support Brazil regardless, but reiterated his wish to see Neymar at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: JOTA Principal: Mendonça prorroga CPMI do INSS e fala em ‘omissão’ da cúpula do Congresso
item.description: Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça granted a preliminary injunction extending the INSS CPMI, giving Senate President Davi Alcolumbre 48 hours to read the extension request — a step sufficient to renew the investigation. He criticized Congress leadership for its 'omission' in failing to advance the probe. Senator Carlos Viana, CPMI president, praised the ruling as a constitutional victory and plans to call new witnesses, including bank representatives linked to payroll loan complaints.

item.title: Lula deve sancionar PL Antifacção nesta terça-feira
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is expected to sign the Anti-Organized Crime Bill on Tuesday (24), which increases penalties for criminal organization participation and introduces new offenses such as 'structured social domain'. The bill includes provisions for extraordinary asset seizure without conviction and voting restrictions for pretrial detainees, drawing criticism from human rights groups over potential constitutional violations.

item.title: Senador bolsonarista é favorito para governar o estado mais forte do agronegócio, diz pesquisa
item.description: Senator Wellington Fagundes (PL), an ally of Jair Bolsonaro, is the favorite to govern Mato Grosso in 2026, according to Real Time Big Data poll, with 37% to 43% voting intention in the first round. He leads in all second-round scenarios, defeating opponents by margins of up to 35 percentage points. Other right-aligned candidates — Otaviano Pivetta (Republicanos), Jayme Campos (União Brasil) and Natasha Shlessarenko (PSD) — trail behind, with Natasha being the only one showing recent rapprochement with the Lula government.

item.title: Decisões de Ratinho e Castro visam salvar sucessores e podem prejudicar PL, avaliam bolsonaristas
item.description: Ratinho Júnior abandoned his presidential pre-candidacy to focus on reelection in Paraná, while Cláudio Castro resigned as Rio de Janeiro governor to avoid potential ineligibility. Bolsonarists criticize Ratinho for underestimating the PL, which strengthened its position by federating Sergio Moro to support Flávio Bolsonaro and weaken the PSD. The strategy aims to secure allied successors and maintain political control in both states amid electoral risks.

item.title: Globo de Cristal coroa temporada perfeita de Lucas Braathen pelo Brasil
item.description: Lucas Pinheiro Braathen won the Giant Slalom Crystal Globe at the FIS Alpine Ski World Cup in Lillehammer, Norway, becoming the first Brazilian ever to claim the title in the discipline. With 547 points, he overtook Swiss skier Marco Odermatt, who crashed out of the first run, securing the overall victory. The triumph capped a historic season in which Braathen also won Brazil’s first-ever Winter Olympic gold medal at Milano-Cortina 2026.

item.title: Sem imunidade e sob pressão do calendário eleitoral, delação de Vorcaro vira xadrez no STF e na PF
item.description: PGR and PF investigators are negotiating terms for a potential plea bargain by Daniel Vorcaro, stressing there is zero chance of criminal immunity and that any deal must bring new, verifiable facts. There is concern about not tying the process to the electoral calendar to avoid accusations of political interference, with assessments that delays could push the agreement past the elections. In the STF, the case is viewed as an institutional chess game, with possible referral to the full plenary by Minister André Mendonça, which could shift the voting balance.

item.title: Após abandono de Ratinho Junior, Caiado viaja a SP para decidir futuro presidencial com Kassab
item.description: Following Ratinho Junior's unexpected withdrawal from the PSD's presidential race, Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado flew to São Paulo to discuss with Gilberto Kassab whether he will become the party's new presidential candidate for 2026. Caiado seeks to confirm the party's genuine backing, having previously been treated as a secondary option, while Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite remains a fallback alternative. The episode has reset the PSD's candidate selection process to an early stage, despite prior investments in infrastructure for Junior's campaign.

item.title: Os motivos para o recuo de Ratinho Jr. da disputa pela presidência
item.description: Ratinho Jr. abandoned his presidential candidacy to avoid losing control of Paraná amid the threat posed by Sergio Moro and the lack of unity in the PSD, which is already signaling support for Lula in parts of the country. His decision prioritized securing a state successor and maintaining his local political base over entering an uncertain and weakened national race.
